title: Identity, Permissions, Ownership, umask, and ACLs · System Forensics
summary: Create a shared operations directory that grants required access without world-writable shortcuts or unexplained privilege escalation. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.
Calculate read, write, and execute effects for owner, group, and other on regular files and directories.
Translate symbolic and octal chmod operations while preserving unrelated bits and verifying the result.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.

Inspect the User Group Identity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.

content.text: For User Group Identity, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the User Group Identity precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A username alone determines every permission decision.

stimulus.title: User Group Identity case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A username alone determines every permission decision.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence. Artifact under review: a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies User Group Identity, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use setgid directories, sticky bit, setuid risk, and posix acl masks only when the base ownership model is insufficient.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe Linux operations investigation headed "# Evidence: linux-special-bits-acls-53a8mv" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Special Bits Acls into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Special Bits Acls: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use setgid directories, sticky bit, setuid risk, and POSIX ACL masks only when the base ownership model is insufficient.
